textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Agent or chatbot should be responsible for handling MFA and dealing with potential multiple MFA devices

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Gateway uses groovy filter to translate Authorize deny to JSON RPC response

textAreas.label: Text
Text
textAreas.text: Authorize can be responsible for the 'final' token exchange

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Authorize protects tools/list discovery checking its client cred token

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: MCP servers MUST validate that tokens presented to them were specifically issued for their use... When an MCP server doesn't verify that tokens were specifically intended for it (for example, via the aud claim), it breaks a fundamental OAuth security boundary."

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: I tried this.. but the issue Glen presents makes it almost not worth it.. You need to use DV and that add compexity.. May as well just let the agent deal with this..

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Token passthrough. If the MCP server not only accepts tokens with incorrect audiences but also forwards these unmodified tokens to downstream services, it can potentially cause the 'confused deputy' problem... [MCP] servers MUST NOT accept or pass tokens that were not explicitly issued for their own services.

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text:
Token:
sub: user1
aud: mcp-gateway.bxf.com
scope: balance
may_act: agent1

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: What if the user doesn't have a device or has multiple. Device selection via DaVinci?
